name: Got Credit? - Credit Repair
description_short: Got Credit? disputes inaccurate items on credit reports across all three bureaus while providing credit coaching and monitoring. Operating for 15+ years at $100/month with no upfront fees.
description_long: Got Credit? has been operating for over 15 years as a credit repair service helping consumers challenge negative items on their credit reports. The company positions itself as a comprehensive credit repair and education provider, not just a dispute service. Their core business model involves disputing inaccurate information with Equifax, Experian, and TransUnion on behalf of clients, while pairing this with credit coaching and financial guidance. The company claims to help clients remove collections, charge-offs, repossessions, and bankruptcies from their reports. Got Credit? operates with transparent pricing at $100 per month with $0 down to enroll, and emphasizes having no upfront fees or hidden gimmicks. They back their service with a 6-month guarantee, suggesting confidence in their dispute process. What distinguishes Got Credit? is their stated emphasis on education and long-term credit building alongside dispute work. They provide one-on-one credit coaching, monthly support via multiple channels (email, text, phone), a secure client portal for tracking progress, and custom recommendations based on individual credit profiles. The company also claims to help clients establish new lines of credit, reduce debt, and prepare for major financial milestones like home purchases or auto loans. They position credit coaching as ongoing rather than transactional. However, important caveats exist. Credit repair disputes cannot legally remove accurate negative information, only challenge items that are inaccurate or unverifiable. The website makes broad claims about helping clients "get approved every time you apply" and "rebuild your future," which overpromise what credit repair alone can achieve. The 6-month guarantee is not clearly defined—it's unclear whether this means full refunds if disputes are unsuccessful or simply a commitment period. No independent reviews or specific success rate data appear on the website, making it difficult to verify their claimed effectiveness. Additionally, consumers can dispute items themselves for free through the credit bureaus, meaning the value proposition depends entirely on whether professional dispute management and coaching justify the $1,200 annual cost.

pricing.guarantee_details: Contact provider for current pricing and guarantee details.

pros: No upfront fees and clear $100/month pricing with $0 down enrollment
Disputes sent to all three major credit bureaus on client's behalf
Includes one-on-one credit coaching alongside dispute services
6-month guarantee backs their service (terms not fully detailed)
24/7 secure client portal access to track disputes and score changes
Monthly support available via email, text, and phone
15+ year operating history suggests established business model
cons: Website makes overclaimed promises like 'get approved every time you apply' which credit repair alone cannot guarantee
6-month guarantee terms are vague and no refund policy details are specified
No independent reviews, testimonials, or verifiable success rates provided on website
Consumers can dispute inaccurate items themselves for free, making $1,200/year cost potentially unnecessary
Cannot remove accurate negative information regardless of service quality, limiting effectiveness for many consumers
best_for: Consumers with verifiably inaccurate negative items on credit reports who prefer professional representation
People seeking credit education and coaching alongside dispute services to build long-term financial habits
Individuals applying for major loans (mortgage, auto, business) who need faster professional dispute support
Those willing to pay for convenience and ongoing support rather than self-disputing with credit bureaus
diagnosis: Got Credit? is best suited for consumers with documentably inaccurate negative items who want professional dispute representation and ongoing credit coaching. The main caveat is that credit repair services cannot remove accurate negative information—effectiveness depends entirely on how many disputed items are actually inaccurate or unverifiable, and consumers should verify before paying $1,200 annually what they can do free themselves.
services: Credit disputes sent to all three credit bureaus (Equifax, Experian, TransUnion)
Monthly support via email, text, and phone
Custom recommendations based on credit profile
Help establishing new lines of credit
Preparation assistance for major financial milestones
typical_results_timeline: Results vary by individual situation. Contact the provider to discuss expected timelines for your specific needs.
address: 331 Newman Springs Rd #143, Red Bank, NJ 07701
